description: An aquatic animal that breathes through gills and moves through water with fins. 'Shalu' names the creatures of rivers, lakes, and seas—beings perfectly adapted to their liquid world, moving with grace through depths humans cannot naturally inhabit.
sound_symbolism: The word flows like water. 'Sha' /ʃa/ begins with hushing flow, water moving. Then 'lu' glides with liquid ease, the fish swimming through its element.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo shalu' (the fish). Takes the animate classifier 'himo' when counted. Can specify type or habitat: 'lo phialu shalu' (river fish). Used both for individual fish and as a category.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fish represent the abundance of waters and the mysteries of the deep—beings of an element that sustains yet remains partly unknowable.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Fish embody flow and adaptation, moving effortlessly with currents rather than fighting against them.
